generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, properties, constants) visible in the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) have correct types (int, real, bool) matching the expressions they define.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` to define helper expressions and `const` to define named constants.
Range checks in AGREE typically use boolean expressions like `value >= lower_limit and value <= upper_limit`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers (types, properties) into AGREE that are not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within component implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to repair AGREE syntax by modifying the AADL architecture.
